ground_truth_fix: In the C world you are casting the pointer in PARAM2 to a (struct socket *) but the verifier does not see that. It only sees that you took a number but it doesn't know that type it is or even that it is a pointer. So you can't dereference using *newsock, you need to do it via bpf_probe_read_kernel. When you write &newsock->type this translates into netsock + offsetof(newsock.type) because you take the address of the dereferenced field. So no actual memory is read, that is valid. But when you write newsock->sk without taking the address you actually perform a memory lookup which isn't allowe...

candidate_repairs.patch_hint: Re-derive the pointer from a verified base immediately before dereference.
candidate_repairs.rationale: The required scalar deref proof was never established.

evidence.detail: The required scalar deref proof was never established.
Verifier symptom: R6 invalid mem access 'inv'
Branch at insn 1 appears to establish a proof: if r6 == 0x0 goto pc+27
Root cause localized to insn 13, earlier than the reject site.
